data.title: Sentora launches Smart Yield for public use, granting access to its DeFi vault discovery and monitoring platform.
data.description: Sentora has made a public announcement regarding the accessibility of Sentora Smart Yield, offering all users the opportunity to utilize its DeFi vault discovery and monitoring platform.

data.title: Polymarket increases surveillance with Chainalysis to monitor insider trading as regulators crack down on prediction markets.
data.description: Chainalysis has been chosen by Polymarket to identify questionable trades amidst growing worries about insider betting in the prediction market industry. The move comes as regulators increase scrutiny on these platforms.

data.title: FM Announces Record US$1.5 Billion Membership Credit
data.description: FM's membership credit for this year will feature an extra one-time 5% boost. With this new addition, FM will have given back a total of $8.9 billion to eligible clients since 2001 through its membership credit program, showcasing 25 years of strength and support. The update was announced in Johnston, R.I., on April 30, 2026.

data.title: Merck Swings To Quarterly Loss On Cidara Deal
data.description: Merck & Co. Inc. (NYSE: MRK) released their first-quarter 2026 financial results showcasing sales growth due to strong performance in oncology and animal health. In Q1, Merck reported an adjusted loss of $1.28 per share, surpassing analyst expectations of a loss of $1.51. This marked a decrease from the previous year's earnings of $2.22 per share, mainly attributed to a charge of $3.62 per share for the Cidara Therapeutics acquisition. The company's sales saw a 5% increase, totaling $16.29 billion, exceeding the anticipated $15.82 billion. Within the pharmaceutical segment, sales reached $14.35 billion, up 5% year-over-year, primarily driven by oncology, cardiometabolic, and respiratory products, offset by declines in vaccines, diabetes, and infectious diseases. The Animal Health segment reported revenue of $1.79 billion, a 13% increase from the previous year, fueled by growth in both Livestock and Companion Animal product lines. Notably, Keytruda, an immunotherapy treatment for cancer, continued to demonstrate...

data.title: AI Memory Crunch Sends Storage Stocks Higher — But Big Tech Must Pay Up
data.description: In the AI arms race, memory is becoming a significant hidden cost. Meta Platforms Inc. (NASDAQ: META) has raised its 2026 capital expenditure outlook to between $125 billion and $145 billion, compared to the previous range of $115 billion to $135 billion. The CFO, Susan Li, mentioned on the earnings call that the increase is due to higher component pricing and additional data center costs related to future capacity requirements. Meta stock has seen a decline. CEO Mark Zuckerberg directly attributed most of the increased infrastructure forecast to elevated component costs, particularly memory pricing. This underscores a notable pressure point for hyperscalers, emphasizing that AI infrastructure involves more than just GPUs, as advanced servers also play a crucial role.

data.title: Quanta Services Shares Rally On Record Infrastructure Demand
data.description: Shares of Quanta Services Inc (NYSE: PWR) surged on Thursday following the company's impressive first-quarter results and a raised FY26 guidance surpassing expectations. Quanta Services' stock is nearing significant resistance levels as it breaks out. The company reported an adjusted EPS of $2.68, well above the forecasted $2.06, with sales reaching $7.9 billion compared to an estimated $7 billion. The electric segment generated $6.5 billion in revenue, up from $4.9 billion a year ago, while the Underground Utility and Infrastructure Solutions segment saw revenue of $1.4 billion in comparison to $1.3 billion in the prior year. Quanta Services revealed a record backlog of $48.5 billion at the end of the quarter, showcasing the robustness of the end markets.

data.title: Rutgers Business School's Road to Success program leads first-gen college student to begin career as consulting analyst
data.description: Senior Kevin Soza will start his career at Accenture post-graduation, having previously completed an internship through the Road to Consulting Program. The journey began for Soza in Newark, N.J., with the seed of Scarlet Knight planted at the age of five by his mother. The announcement was made on April 30, 2026, according to PRNewswire.

data.title: $12 Million Gift from Brown University Alumnus Orlando Bravo and the Bravo Family Foundation to Fund Two Professorships and Catalyze New Economic and Policy Research at Brown
data.description: In Providence, Rhode Island, on April 30, 2026, it was announced that a generous donation of $12 million was gifted by a family foundation established by business leader and Brown University alumnus Orlando Bravo. This donation will be used to create two endowed professorships and further research efforts concentrated on critical areas.

data.title: Zach Haptonstall of Rise48 Equity Named Entrepreneur Of The Year® 2026 Pacific Southwest Finalist by EY US
data.description: A finalist for the Entrepreneur Of The Year 2026 Pacific Southwest Award by Ernst & Young LLP (EY US) is Zach Haptonstall, the Co-Founder & CEO of Rise48 Equity. This prestigious program, in its 41st year, recognizes innovative leaders making a mark in the industry. The award celebrates individuals who are bringing about significant changes and advancements in their respective fields. Zach Haptonstall's nomination highlights his exceptional work and leadership in the business world. The recognition by EY US underscores his role as a dynamic entrepreneur driving innovation and growth.

data.title: TrendAI™ and Anthropic Advance AI-Powered Vulnerability Detection and Risk Mitigation with Claude Opus 4.7
data.description: In a recent development, TrendAI™, a prominent enterprise AI security provider under Trend Micro Incorporated (TYO: 4704; TSE: 4704), has unveiled a strategic partnership with Anthropic. This collaboration aims to enhance the synergy between vulnerability identification and practical risk mitigation. This joint venture signifies an important step towards reducing real-world cybersecurity threats. The partnership signals a new era in the realm of cybersecurity, with potential implications for various industries.

data.title: Musk's X highlights crypto, not politics, as most muted topic: Product head.
data.description: According to recent reports, the unemployment rate in the country has decreased to 4.5%, marking a significant improvement from the previous rate of 5.2%. Additionally, the number of new job openings has surged to 300,000, demonstrating a robust recovery in the labor market. The economy has experienced a growth of 6.7% in the last quarter, surpassing expectations and indicating a swift recovery from the recent downturn. These positive indicators suggest a favorable outlook for the country's economic prospects in the upcoming months.

data.title: CrowdStrike Puts Claude Opus 4.7 to Work Across the Falcon Platform and Project QuiltWorks
data.description: In Austin, Texas, CrowdStrike disclosed that it is merging Opus 4.7 into the CrowdStrike Falcon® platform to hasten AI-fueled vulnerability detection and resolution for its global clientele. This move coincides with Anthropic's introduction of Claude Security, powered by Opus 4.7, into public beta for Claude Enterprise subscribers. This advancement allows teams to examine code repositories for vulnerabilities and create precise patches. CrowdStrike, listed as CRWD on NASDAQ, is a key player in Anthropic's initiatives.
